_comment: Single source of truth for analysis request-key string format. Consumed by both the JS deriver test (src/analysis/analysisRequestKeyFormat.test.js) and the Rust validator test (src-tauri/src/ipc/commands.rs). Changing the key grammar on either side without updating this file fails one of those tests. Format: spectrum pair = spectrum:pair:<x>:<y>:<view>:sp<speedPercent>:tilt<tiltCentidb>:sm<octaveSmoothingToken> (view in combined|lr|ms); spectrum single = spectrum:single:<ch>:combined:sp<speedPercent>:tilt<tiltCentidb>:sm<octaveSmoothingToken> (view always forced to combined); vectorscope = vectorscope:pair:<x>:<y>. sp is Speed, the time axis; sm is octave smoothing, the frequency axis. octaveSmoothing carries the wire value (off|1/12|1/6|1/3) while its key token drops the fraction (off|12|6|3), so no '/' can enter the colon-delimited grammar.
_wirePayloadComment: One complete set_analysis_requests payload, exactly as the JS runtime sends it. Guards the request SHAPE, which the key fixtures above do not: they only pin the key string. The JS test asserts deriveBackendAnalysisRequests emits this object; the Rust test asserts it deserializes into AnalysisRequests. SpectrumAnalysisRequest declares no serde defaults, so a field dropped on the JS side fails the whole payload and blanks every analysis panel — vectorscope included — not just the one that lost the field.
